user_query:
Please create persona documentation for our new developer-focused product, LogLamp. Read these source files for context: input/product_overview.md, input/landing.md, input/customer_research.md, and input/references/template.md. Produce two persona docs and an index under output/:

1) output/docs/personas/backend_engineer.md — Primary persona for "Backend Engineer at a mid-size SaaS"
2) output/docs/personas/sre_manager.md — Secondary persona for "SRE Manager"
3) output/docs/PERSONA.md — Index that links to both personas

Follow the template structure and these requirements for each persona file:
- Title line: "# LogLamp - Persona: <Persona Name>"
- Include the following sections with clear headings in this exact order: "## Target User", "## Problems Solved", "## Discovery", "## Onboarding", "## User Journey", "## Feature Touchpoints"
- In Onboarding, include lines that literally contain the phrases "Time to value" and "First success moment"
- In User Journey, include subsections with headings: "### New User (Day 1)", "### Returning User (Week 1)", "### Power User (Month 1+)"
- Include at least one evidence quote as a blockquote line starting with ">" that reflects insights from input/customer_research.md
- Mention these product-specific keywords at least once in each persona file: OpenTelemetry, structured logging, Kubernetes, alerts, SLIs/SLOs
- Include a Feature Touchpoints markdown table with a header row "| Feature | When Encountered | User Need at That Moment |" and at least two data rows
- For the SRE Manager persona, also mention either "compliance" or "audit"

For the index file output/docs/PERSONA.md:
- Start with the title "# Personas"
- Include bullet links to both personas using relative paths: "- [Backend Engineer](personas/backend_engineer.md)" and "- [SRE Manager](personas/sre_manager.md)"

Keep the writing specific and actionable, reflecting real users and actual use cases described in the inputs.
